title: Saudi Job Sources Registry
description: Validates data/saudi-job-sources.json. Each source entry represents a job platform relevant to the Saudi labor market.

version.description: Semantic version of this registry file.

last_reviewed.description: ISO 8601 date of the last manual review.

status_values.description: Enumerated values permitted in the status field of each source entry.

scraping_complexity_values.description: Enumerated values permitted in the scraping_complexity field of each source entry.

saudi_relevance_values.description: Enumerated values permitted in the saudi_relevance field of each source entry.

sources.description: Registry of job sources. Each entry represents one platform or portal.

id.description: Unique identifier. Lowercase kebab-case only. Used as a stable reference key across the system.

name.description: Human-readable display name of the platform or portal.

category.description: Classification of the source by type. Extend this enum when adding entries in new categories.

website.$comment: Accepts a URI string or null. Null is used when the platform has no unified public URL (e.g., Taleo deployments are employer-specific).

items.description: ISO 639-1 language code. Extend the enum if additional languages are encountered.

language_support.description: Languages in which this platform serves job content.

authentication_required.description: Whether accessing job listings requires an authenticated session.

scraping_complexity.$comment: The metadata field in the data file lists 'not_applicable' as a valid value. This schema uses 'unknown' instead for semantic clarity. Align the metadata field when this schema is adopted as authoritative.
scraping_complexity.description: Relative difficulty of automated data extraction. 'low' = static HTML with stable structure. 'medium' = dynamic content or pagination required. 'high' = authentication, rate limiting, or legal constraints. 'unknown' = not assessed.

saudi_relevance.$comment: Values use primary/secondary/peripheral to match the existing dataset. A future normalization to high/medium/low is possible but requires migrating all entries.
saudi_relevance.description: How directly relevant this source is for Saudi market job intelligence. 'primary' = core Saudi source. 'secondary' = regional or international with meaningful Saudi content. 'peripheral' = limited Saudi-specific content.

status.description: Operational status of this registry entry. 'active' = known to be live and usable. 'planned' = identified but not yet integrated. 'deprecated' = no longer relevant. 'unverified' = existence confirmed but details not validated.

notes.description: Contextual notes on the source: hiring patterns, access constraints, content characteristics, and Saudi market relevance. Must be substantive.
